import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut nums: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  mut cur = 0
  mut sign = 1
  mut has = false
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(b)
      if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 10 or b == 13
        if has
          nums = list.push(nums, cur * sign)
          cur = 0
          sign = 1
          has = false
        end
      else if b == 45
        sign = -1
      else
        cur = cur * 10 + (b - 48)
        has = true
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if has
    nums = list.push(nums, cur * sign)
  end
  nums
end

fn remove_at(_ xs: List<Int>, _ idx: Int) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = list.len(xs)
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    if i != idx
      match list.get(xs, i)
      when Some(v)
        result = list.push(result, v)
      when None
        ()
      end
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  result
end

let line1 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let line2 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let nums = parse_ints(line1)

let k = match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
when Some(n)
  n
when None
  0
end

let total = list.len(nums)
let take = if k < total
  k
else
  total
end

mut remaining = nums
mut output = ""
mut count = 0

while count < take
  match list.max(remaining)
  when Some(m)
    match list.index_of(remaining, m)
    when Some(idx)
      remaining = remove_at(remaining, idx)
    when None
      ()
    end
    if count == 0
      output = "#{m}"
    else
      output = "#{output} #{m}"
    end
  when None
    ()
  end
  count = count + 1
end

println(output)
